recipes.title: Bariis Iskukaris
recipes.description: Fragrant Somali spiced rice cooked with goat or beef, caramelised onions, and xawaash spice blend.
steps: Rinse the rice in cold water until clear, then soak for 20 minutes and drain.
Heat the oil in a large pot and fry the onions until deeply caramelised, about 15 minutes.
Add the garlic and xawaash, stir for 1 minute, then add the goat pieces and brown all over.
Stir in the tomatoes and tomato paste and cook for 5 minutes.
Pour in 700 ml of the water, season with 1.5 tsp of the salt, cover, and simmer until the meat is tender, about 60 minutes.
Lift out the meat and set aside. Top up the broth to 750 ml total liquid.
Add the rice and remaining salt, stir once, and lay the meat back on top.
Cover tightly, lower the heat to minimum, and steam for 20 minutes.
Scatter raisins over the rice, rest off heat for 10 minutes, then fluff and serve.

recipes.title: Sambusa
recipes.description: Crisp Somali fried pastry triangles filled with spiced minced beef, onion, and green chilli.
steps: Heat the 2 tbsp oil in a pan and sauté the onion until soft.
Add the garlic and chilli and cook for 1 minute.
Add the ground beef and xawaash, breaking up the meat, and cook until browned and dry, about 12 minutes.
Stir in the coriander and salt, then cool the filling completely.
Whisk the flour and water into a thick paste to use as glue.
Fold each pastry strip into a cone, fill with a heaping tablespoon of filling, fold over to seal into a triangle, and brush the seam with the flour paste.
Heat the frying oil to 170°C.
Fry the sambusas in batches for 3-4 minutes until deep golden, turning halfway.
Drain on paper towels and serve hot, especially during Ramadan iftar.

recipes.title: Maraq
recipes.description: Soothing Somali soup of meat and vegetables in a tomato-spiced broth, eaten with bread.
steps: Heat the oil in a heavy pot and sauté the onion until softened.
Add the garlic and xawaash and cook briefly until fragrant.
Add the lamb and brown on all sides, about 8 minutes.
Stir in the tomatoes and tomato paste and cook until they break down, about 5 minutes.
Pour in the water, bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer covered for 45 minutes.
Add the carrots and potato and continue simmering for 20 minutes until the meat and vegetables are tender.
Season with salt to taste.
Stir in the chopped coriander and serve hot in bowls with anjero or muufo.

recipes.title: Anjero
recipes.description: Light, lacy Somali fermented pancake similar to injera, eaten for breakfast with stew or sugar and ghee.
steps: In a large bowl, dissolve the yeast and sugar in 200 ml of the warm water and let stand for 10 minutes until foamy.
Whisk in the remaining water, sorghum flour, and all-purpose flour until smooth and lump-free.
Cover and leave at room temperature for 6-8 hours until bubbly and pleasantly sour.
Whisk in the salt and adjust consistency with a little extra water if needed; the batter should be like thin pancake batter.
Heat a non-stick pan over medium heat with no oil.
Pour a ladle of batter and swirl in a spiral from the centre outward to make a thin circle.
Cover with a lid and cook for 1-2 minutes until the surface is fully covered with small holes and the edges lift; do not flip.
Slide onto a clean cloth and stack as you go. Serve warm with stew, or with sesame oil and sugar.

recipes.title: Soor
recipes.description: Somali maize porridge similar to ugali, served with stew, milk, or ghee depending on the meal.
steps: Bring the water to a rolling boil in a heavy pot and add the salt.
Reduce to medium heat and gradually rain in the maize flour while stirring vigorously with a wooden spoon to prevent lumps.
Continue stirring as the porridge thickens, about 5 minutes.
Lower the heat, cover, and let steam for 5 minutes.
Uncover, stir again, and cook another 5 minutes pressing the soor against the sides of the pot.
Once the dough pulls cleanly away from the pot, fold in the ghee.
Wet a bowl, scoop the soor in, and invert onto a serving plate to form a smooth mound.
Serve hot with maraq or with milk and sugar for breakfast.

recipes.title: Hilib Ari
recipes.description: Slow-simmered Somali goat meat stew with onions, tomatoes, and warming xawaash.
steps: Pat the goat pieces dry and season with salt.
Heat the oil in a heavy pot and brown the goat in batches until deeply coloured, then remove.
Add the onions to the same pot and cook until softened and golden, about 10 minutes.
Stir in the garlic, ginger, and xawaash and cook for 1 minute.
Add the tomatoes and tomato paste and cook until they break down into a sauce.
Return the goat and any juices, pour in the water, and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 90 minutes until the meat is fall-off-the-bone tender, topping up water if needed.
Uncover and reduce the sauce for 10 minutes until thickened and glossy.
Stir in the coriander and serve with bariis or anjero.

recipes.title: Suqaar
recipes.description: Quick Somali sauté of finely diced beef or lamb with onions, peppers, and xawaash.
steps: Heat the oil in a wide skillet over high heat until shimmering.
Add the diced beef and sear without moving for 2 minutes to develop colour.
Stir and cook another 3 minutes until the beef is browned but still juicy.
Add the onion and bell pepper and stir-fry for 4 minutes until just tender.
Add the garlic and xawaash and cook for 1 minute.
Stir in the tomato and cook for 2 minutes more until lightly broken down.
Season with salt and pepper, fold in the coriander, and serve immediately with bariis, baasto, or anjero.

recipes.title: Baasto
recipes.description: Somali pasta with spiced beef sauce and a banana on the side, an Italian-influenced national favourite.
steps: Heat the oil in a saucepan and sauté the onion until softened.
Add the garlic and xawaash and cook for 1 minute.
Add the ground beef and cook, breaking it up, until browned, about 8 minutes.
Stir in the tomato paste and grated carrot and cook for 3 minutes.
Add the chopped tomatoes and water, season with salt, and simmer uncovered for 25 minutes until thickened.
Meanwhile, cook the spaghetti in salted boiling water until al dente, then drain.
Toss the spaghetti with about a quarter of the sauce until coated.
Plate the pasta, ladle more sauce on top, and serve with a peeled ripe banana on the side.

recipes.title: Cambuulo
recipes.description: Slow-cooked Somali adzuki bean and rice dish, finished with sesame oil and sugar for breakfast or iftar.
steps: Soak the adzuki beans overnight in plenty of cold water, then drain.
Place the beans in a large pot, cover with the fresh water, and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat and simmer, partially covered, for about 75 minutes until the beans are very tender, topping up water as needed.
Add the rice and salt, stir, and continue simmering for 20-25 minutes until the rice is fully cooked and the mixture is thick and porridge-like.
If the mixture is too dry, stir in a little hot water; if too loose, simmer uncovered to reduce.
Spoon into bowls while hot.
Top each bowl with a generous drizzle of sesame oil and a spoonful of sugar.
Serve immediately, especially during Ramadan iftar.
